channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: What to know about OpenAI’s new, free AI training course
item.description: OpenAI and nonprofit partner Common Sense Media have launched a free training course for teachers aimed at demystifying artificial intelligence and prompt engineering, the organizations said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Google Unveils First Mass Timber Office Building in Sunnyvale
item.description: Google's innovative office building in Sunnyvale showcases sustainable architecture, eco-friendly design, and cutting-edge mass timber construction techniques.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks, once star performers for the entertainment giant
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast will spin off many of its cable television networks that were once at the heart of the entertainment giant, with people increasingly swapping out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety, Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2024 — get the latest news and insights
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 kicks off in Chicago and runs Nov. 19-22. If you can’t make it to Chicago, no worries. First, the physical event is sold out, according to the Ignite event page. Second, it’s a hybrid event, so you can attend Ignite virtually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: An Android-ChromeOS merger might actually make sense now
item.description: Stop me if you’ve heard this one: Google’s got two primary user-facing platforms, Android and ChromeOS — and, if the latest rumors are to be believed, the company may be on the brink of bringing ’em together and combining ’em into a single, streamlined entity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta creates ‘Business AI’ group led by ex-Salesforce AI CEO Clara Shih
item.description: Meta has hired the former CEO of Salesforce AI, Clara Shih, to lead a new “Business AI” group. Salesforce has still not publicly acknowledged Shih’s departure, but it will be hard to ignore now that she’s announced her new role on her LinkedIn profile.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Business Internet Security: Everything You Need to Consider
item.description: Cyber-attacks can cost companies millions of dollars in lost revenue, legal fees, and recovery efforts. A security breach can severely tarnish a company’s reputation and customer trust, making comprehensive internet security crucial for your small business.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Government commission proposes Manhattan Project-style AI funding plan
item.description: A U.S. congressional commission proposed a "Manhattan Project-like" initiative for the funding of artificial intelligence (AI) development in a larger push to stay ahead of China's technological advancements. The report, issued Tuesday by the bipartisan U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Cato Networks adds TLS inspection capabilities to SASE platform
item.description: Secure access service edge provider Cato Networks today announced it added transport layer security inspection capabilities to its SASE platform to enable customers to inspect TLS traffic more easily and quickly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft brings automated ‘agents’ to M365 Copilot
item.description: Microsoft has introduced a new tool in Microsoft 365 Copilot to automate repetitive tasks, part of a drive to make the generative AI (genAI) assistant more useful to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German police break up an environmental activists' camp near Tesla plant outside Berlin
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German police broke up a protest camp housing environmental activists in a forest near the Tesla electric car factory in Grünheide outside Berlin on Tuesday, citing violations of public safety and order.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Library of Congress says an adversary hacked some emails
item.description: The library said that an adversary accessed email communications during the period from January to September.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity
item.description: If left unchecked, Microsoft’s practices will become the industry standard, clearing the path for other providers to harm customers and weaken their defenses. The post Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 21 proposed features.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: NASA Establishes New Class of Astrophysics Missions, Selects Studies
item.description: NASA selects two proposals for missions to observe X-ray and far-infrared wavelengths of light from space for additional review The post NASA Establishes New Class of Astrophysics Missions, Selects Studies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network
